votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the factorial and sum_1_to_n functions, and the f_spec function accurately checks the length and the alternating sequence conditions specified in the Coq code.

The Python code correctly implements the factorial and sum functions, and the f_spec function checks the length and element-wise conditions (factorial for even 1-based indices, sum for odd) exactly as specified in the Coq code.
The Python code correctly implements the factorial and sum_1_to_n functions, and the f_spec accurately checks the length and the alternating sequence conditions as specified in the Coq code.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. factorial and sum_1_to_n match their Coq counterparts. f_spec checks that the result has length n and that for each index i < n, if logical_index (i+1) is even, the value equals factorial(logical_index), otherwise it equals sum_1_to_n(logical_index). The Nat.even check in Coq corresponds to logical_index % 2 == 0 in Python. The nth function with default 0 is handled correctly since i < len(result) is always true when i < n and len(result) == n.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. factorial and sum_1_to_n are identical recursive functions. f_spec checks the same conditions: length equals n, and for each index i, if logical_index (i+1) is even, the value must equal factorial(logical_index), otherwise it must equal sum_1_to_n(logical_index). The Python uses logical_index % 2 == 0 which matches Coq's Nat.even for the even check. The nth with default 0 is handled correctly since i < n guarantees i < len(result).

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. factorial and sum_1_to_n match their Coq counterparts. f_spec checks that the result has length n and that for each index i < n, if logical_index (i+1) is even, the value equals factorial(logical_index), otherwise it equals sum_1_to_n(logical_index). The Nat.even check in Coq corresponds to logical_index % 2 == 0 in Python. Both return the same concrete judgments for all inputs.

Matches Coq: same factorial and sum recursions, checks length equality, uses 1-based index parity to select value, and accesses the i-th element consistently with nth; behavior aligns for all natural-number inputs.

Python mirrors the Coq definitions: factorial and sum_1_to_n match, f_spec enforces length=n and checks each 1-based index with the same even/odd rule. The nth default is never used due to the length check. Thus for all natural n, the judgments coincide.

factorial and sum_1_to_n match the Coq definitions, and f_spec enforces the same length and per-index (1-based) parity-based checks; nth’s default is unused since i<n=length, so behavior aligns on all nat inputs.
